segments.title: Selling America on sea power
segments.summary: The lecture traces how Roosevelt, Mahan, Sims, and later Benjamin Armstrong argued for American sea power and naval policy.
segments.body: The discussion captures the episode's organizing argument. Dr. Clarke frames the lecture as a study of how America was sold on sea power, moving from Roosevelt's use of War of 1812 memory through Mahan's pressure for maritime strategy and Sims's post-World War I balanced-fleet case.

evidence.note: The introduction states the topic as how to sell America on sea power and lays out Roosevelt, Mahan, Sims, and Benjamin Armstrong as the organizing sequence.
The transition from Roosevelt to Mahan says Mahan built on the battle-fleet case and pressed against inward-looking policy.
The conclusion says Mahan connects sea power and national strategy, then contrasts British naval theorists with American writers who had to argue for the Navy's existence.
